snippet.title: What Is an Elmer in Ham Radio? (The Person Who Changes Everything)
snippet.description: An Elmer is an experienced ham radio operator who mentors newer hams. They've been where you are — through the exam, the gear confusion, the first contacts. They answer your beginner questions without making you feel like a beginner. The term came from a 1970s QST magazine article and it stuck. Most experienced hams will tell you: their Elmer made the difference. If you have one, hold on to them. And if you need one — reach out to your local ham radio club. Tap subscribe and I'll do my best to be one for you.

snippet.title: This $40 Gadget Turns Your Phone Into a REAL Ham Radio
snippet.description:
Ever wondered if your smartphone could replace a handheld radio?

In this video, I test the KV4P HT, a tiny open-source device that turns your Android phone into a fully functional ham radio—no internet required.

From voice contacts to off-grid texting, this thing claims to do it all… but does it actually work in the real world?

👉 In this video:
 • Real-world testing of the KV4P HT
 • Audio quality and range check
 • Setup and usability
 • Is it worth building or buying?

The KV4P HT is a DIY VHF/UHF transceiver that plugs directly into your Android device and uses it as the interface and power source￼. It’s compact, portable, and designed for off-grid communication—perfect for field ops, emergency use, or just experimenting with something new in ham radio.

⚠️ Requires an amateur radio license to transmit.

👇 Let me know in the comments:
Would you replace your handheld with this?

KV4P HT on Amazon - https://amzn.to/3NMY7ZF

KV4P HT Discord Server - https://discord.gg/frZdb2BCg

snippet.title: Can Homeowners Charge Their Own AC?
snippet.description:
Can Homeowners Charge Their Own AC?

We just replaced a leaking mini split head and are charging it back to factory specs but here’s the real question:

Can a homeowner do this?

Short answer: No, not properly.

Here’s why:
• You need an EPA license to legally purchase refrigerant
• You must charge the system to exact factory weight
• You need a scale and proper tools
• And most importantly  if it’s low, there’s likely a leak

In this case, we’re charging to:
 ~5 lbs 12 oz (based on factory spec)

That precision matters. Too much or too little refrigerant can cause:
• Poor performance
• System damage
• Shortened lifespan

And remember refrigerant doesn’t just disappear.
If you’re adding it, something is wrong.

That’s why this is a job best left to a licensed technician.

snippet.title: Quansheng UV-K1 Hacked: Listening to SSB HF Ham Radio & Air Band (all in one) IOTCU Si4732 Board
snippet.description:
In this video, we take a look at the Quansheng UV-K1 handheld radio running the custom IOTCU firmware (v4.322). We test its expanded receiving capabilities across different bands, including:

- ​HF Band: Monitoring 21.265 MHz, 14.251 MHz, and 7.181 MHz (LSB/USB). [01:00]
- ​FM Radio: Standard broadcast reception at 96.60 MHz. [02:30]
- ​LW/MW Band: Testing 0.225 MHz. [02:48]
- ​VHF/Ham Radio: 144.490 MHz CW beacon reception [03:32]
- ​Air Band: Tuning into Air Radar/Weather frequencies around 133.725 MHz and 134.475 MHz. [04:04]

- Watch the UV-K1 MODIFICATION VIDEO: https://youtu.be/PndkKI9ar1Q
- Buy the Quansheng UV-K1 here: https://s.click.aliexpress.com/e/_c4rryLf7

snippet.title: ¡Le salió humo blanco! Reparando mi viejo roímetro de HF.
snippet.description:
¡LE SALIÓ HUMO! 💨 Reparación y Mejora de mi Roímetro (Diseño ARRL)
¡Hola a todos! Bienvenidos a un nuevo video de Espacio de César. Hoy vamos a abrir la "Caja de Pandora".
Hace un tiempo, en un descuido con un lineal de 1KW, mi fiel roímetro de HF decidió jubilarse con una pequeña nube de humo blanco. En este video, vamos a restaurarlo por completo y mejorar su capacidad de potencia utilizando un diseño clásico que me encanta: el Puente de Wheatstone del manual ARRL Antenna Handbook (Capítulo 27).
Lo mejor de este circuito es su sencillez: no requiere bobinas, toroides ni modificar cables coaxiales. Solo resistencias, capacitores y diodos.
¿Qué vamos a ver en este video?
El Diagnóstico: Identificamos por qué se quemaron las resistencias originales de 1/4W.
La Mejora: Implementamos resistencias de 2W para que el medidor soporte hasta 400 Watts sin despeinarse.
El Montaje: Paso a paso del armado tipo "araña" para evitar capacidades parásitas.
Las Pruebas: * Prueba con carga fantasma casera (100 ohms en paralelo).
Comparativa de medición real con el Kenwood TS-450S.
Auditoría técnica con el NanoVNA para ver el límite de frecuencia (¡sorpresa a los 50MHz!).
Componentes utilizados:
6 resistencias de 100 ohms (2W) para formar las ramas de 50 ohms.
2 resistencias de 47K y 1 de 22K (según el instrumento).
3 capacitores de 5 nF.
2 diodos de germanio o 1N4148 (apareados).
Gabinete de aluminio, conectores SO-239 y microamperímetro de 100uA.
📥 DESCARGAS:
La escala para el microamperímetro que utilicé la pueden descargar aquí: 
https://liberaturadio.org/medidor-de-roe-swr-meter/
Si te gustó el proyecto, no olvides darle Like, Suscribirte y activar la campanita 🔔 para no perderte el próximo video donde armaremos un Sintonizador de Antenas Manual incorporando este mismo roímetro.
Hazte miembro del canal para acceder al grupo exclusivo de WhatsApp y contenido premium!
73 y buenos DX de LU5HJD.

snippet.title: Why You Only Hear Asian Stations on HF in Australia
snippet.description:
Tune around the HF bands in Australia today… and you’ll notice something strange.

Where Australia used to broadcast across the region, you now mostly hear stations coming in from Asia.
So what actually happened?

In this video, I look at the rise and fall of Australian shortwave broadcasting — from the days of Radio Australia to the shutdown of the Australian Broadcasting Corporation shortwave service in 2017.

Shortwave didn’t disappear.
Australia just stopped using it.

Meanwhile, other broadcasters kept going… and that’s exactly what you’re hearing on the bands today.

🎧 What this video covers
Why Australia used HF/shortwave in the first place
The shutdown of ABC shortwave services
Why the bands now sound dominated by Asia
What replaced shortwave in modern Australia

Antenna:
Location: Melbourne, Australia
📚 Sources

snippet.title: Beginner's Guide | Don’t Start SDR Until You Watch This! (RTL-SDR vs HackRF vs Pluto vs Portapack)
snippet.description:
#SDR #RTLSDR #HackRF #PlutoSDR #Wireless #RFEngineering #SpectrumAnalyzer #PortaPack #SoftwareDefinedRadio #HamRadio #SignalProcessing #CyberSecurity #TinySA #USRP #RF

This video is a complete beginner-to-advanced guide to Software Defined Radio (SDR), designed to help you start your SDR journey with the right hardware and understanding.

I walk through real lab equipment including:

-- RTL-SDR (best entry-level receiver)
-- HackRF One + Mayhem PortaPack (portable SDR + signal analysis)
-- ADALM PlutoSDR (RF learning & transceiver platform)
-- USRP (professional-grade SDR systems)
-- TinySA Ultra+ Spectrum Analyzer
-- Essential RF accessories (antennas, SMA adapters, filters)

🔬 What You’ll Learn:
-- SDR fundamentals (RF digitization, IQ sampling)
-- Difference between low-cost and professional SDRs
-- Which SDR YOU should start with (based on budget & goals)
-- Real-world RF lab setup overview
-- Practical signal exploration workflow

⚡ Perfect For:
-- Electrical / RF Engineering students
-- HackRF / PortaPack users
-- Wireless security & spectrum analysis beginners
-- Ham radio enthusiasts

This video gives you a clear roadmap from beginner → advanced SDR systems, eliminating confusion and saving you money.

🔔 Don’t forget to Like & Subscribe to the channel, and click the Bell Icon to stay updated with the latest tutorials, experiments, and SDR-based projects!

📡 Explore more SDR, RF, and wireless communication content on my YouTube channel:👉 @Muhammed_Mustaqim

GitHub Repositories: https://github.com/mmustaqim

🎥 Join me on a journey through Software Defined Radio, Digital Modulation, Wireless Systems, and more practical demonstrations designed for enthusiasts, students, and professionals.

snippet.title: #dublagem
snippet.description:
Here’s what a typical Morse code conversation between radio operators sounds like. It uses short and long audio signals to represent letters and numbers. Most amateur radio operators communicate at speeds of 20 to 40 words per minute. At this pace, an untrained listener only hears high-pitched beeping. But an experienced operator can recognize whole word patterns without decoding each letter individually. It’s a fascinating skill that turns what sounds like random noise into a meaningful conversation.
👍 Like | 🔗 Share | 💬 Comment+For educational and informational purposes only.

snippet.title: How China Lost Its Cheap Iran Oil Edge
snippet.description:
This short tracks how the March 2026 Iran war disrupted China's cheap-oil advantage by combining an OFAC waiver, shadow-fleet logistics, Malaysia's laundering role, and the re-entry of other buyers into the market.

Key points used in the short:
- OFAC General License U temporarily authorized already-loaded Iranian cargoes at sea.
- USCC says China bought upward of 90 percent of Iran's exported oil in 2025, often at an $8 to $10 discount.
- Expert AI inference used in-scene: 1.4 million barrels/day x 365 x $9 midpoint discount = about $4.6 billion in implied 2025 savings.
- UANI and Straits Times material describe Malaysia as a major STS laundering hub, with activity rising from 280 cases in 2023 to 679 in 2025.
- S&P Global says cargoes reached China with Malaysia-origin certificates instead of direct Iranian customs reporting.
- India's government said refiners made their first Iranian oil purchase in seven years after the waiver window opened.
- CSIS argues Beijing had strong incentives to avoid escalation but limited ability to shape events or keep traffic moving.

SOURCES:
- https://ofac.treasury.gov/recent-actions/20260320_33
- https://ofac.treasury.gov/media/935376/download?inline=
- https://www.uscc.gov/research/china-iran-fact-sheet-short-primer-relationship
- https://home.treasury.gov/news/press-releases/sb0275
- https://www.unitedagainstnucleariran.com/blog/january-2026-iran-tanker-tracker
- https://www.unitedagainstnucleariran.com/uani_in_news/malaysia-releases-tankers-suspected-of-transferring-illegal-crude-oil-worth-us129
- https://www.spglobal.com/energy/en/news-research/latest-news/crude-oil/021225-us-targets-iranian-oil-transshipment-to-further-disrupt-cargo-flows-to-china
- https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/international-business/india-makes-first-oil-purchase-from-iran-in-7-years-with-no-payment-hurdle/articleshow/130032606.cms
- https://www.csis.org/analysis/no-one-not-even-beijing-getting-through-strait-hormuz
- https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-13/malaysia-says-its-subsidy-bill-to-rise-due-to-higher-oil-prices

snippet.title: The POTA Performers 🌍 POTA Adventures Around the World! | Gary Morton (M1GRY)
snippet.description:
Join Stuart Crawford (VE9CF) for an incredible conversation with Gary Morton (M1GRY) as they dive deep into the world of Parks on the Air (POTA) and international radio activations!

Whether you are a seasoned activator, a dedicated hunter, or just looking to take your first steps into portable amateur radio, Gary shares a wealth of knowledge from his worldwide experiences. From dodging tornadoes in the US to pulling off 1-watt SSB contacts in Cyprus, this interview is packed with wild stories, budget gear recommendations, and pro tips for all POTA operators.

👇 Don't forget to LIKE, COMMENT, and SUBSCRIBE for more Ham Radio content! 👇

📻 Topics Covered in this Interview:

🔹 Gary’s Radio Journey: Passing his exam at 15, getting his license in 2002, and making the leap from VHF/UHF to HF operating after the UK dropped its Morse code requirement.

🔹 Discovering POTA: Finding the magic of POTA at the 2023 Orlando HamCation—and the hilarious story of his very first (failed) activation.

🔹 US Activations & Wild Weather: Operating across FL, GA, VA, WV, and OH, claiming a historical activation at Antelope Island, UT, and surviving a severe tornado warning on the TN/AL border!

🔹 Operating in Cyprus (5B/M1GRY): How Gary travels light for international pileups, including an amazing 1-watt SSB activation in a Cyprus national forest.

🔹 Crucial International Regulations: Important warnings about Cyprus operations, including strict prohibitions in the UN Green Zone, UK Sovereign Bases and Northern Turkish areas.

🔹 Pro POTA Tips: * Boundary Planning: Why you need tools like "Magic Maps" (UK) and Google Street View to verify legal operating boundaries.

🔹 On-the-Air Advice: The absolute necessity of self-spotting, and why QRP operators need extra patience!

🎒 Gear Mentioned by Gary:

Yaesu FT-817: The classic QRP rig he used to transition into HF.
Icom IC-703: His go-to lightweight transceiver for international travel.
AliExpress Telescopic Antenna: Gary highly recommends this budget-friendly, easy-to-deploy antenna for portable ops across different bands.

🔗 Helpful Links & Resources:

Parks on the Air Official Site: https://parksontheair.com/
Magic Maps (UK Boundary Mapping)
Google Street View (For international boundary scouting)

#HamRadio #AmateurRadio #POTA #ParksOnTheAir #M1GRY #VE9CF #QRP #RadioActivations #HamCation #PortableRadio #CyprusRadio

snippet.title: Kupiłeś XR-136 i masz ciszę na HF? Zobacz jak to naprawić!
snippet.description:
Kupiłem SDR-a, który był „ślepy” na fale krótkie! ❌📡 Zamiast go odsyłać, wziąłem lutownicę i poprawiłem fabrykę. Ten różowy przewód to nie przypadek – to „bajpas”, który ożywił wejście HF w moim XR-136! 🛠️ Teraz w końcu słyszę, co dzieje się na pasmach amatorskich. Chcesz zobaczyć, jak zhakować swój sprzęt? Oglądaj do końca! 🚀

#RadioFanatyk #SDR #HardwareHack #elektronika #krotkofalarstwo #majsterkowanie #DIY #XR136 #techhacks #nasluch

snippet.title: Man Contacts ISS Astronauts From Earth
snippet.description:
An amateur radio operator on Earth successfully made contact with an astronaut aboard the International Space Station. Using a ham radio and directional antenna, signals were transmitted and received in real time as the station passed overhead.

#space #iss #radio #communication #science #shorts
